commits:
[{'sha': '7d0fc1f5161495fd4612d4004bb4bb7c926bc0b9', 'message': 'Render example and default fields in kubectl explain output

CRDs can declare example and default values in their JSON schema, and
these flow through to the OpenAPI v3 endpoint. kubectl explain already
receives this data but the plaintext template never renders it.

Add DEFAULT: and EXAMPLE: sections to both the top-level description
view and the per-field detail view in plaintext.tmpl, following the
same pattern used for externalDocs (PR #136988). Hidden in recursive
mode to keep --recursive output compact.'}]

review:
[{'type': 'Bug', 'conclusion': '修复了在收到429错误后，Pod可能已迁移到其他节点时，仍继续驱逐的问题。', 'description': '在收到驱逐请求的429（TooManyRequests）错误后，代码会休眠并重试。然而，在此期间，Pod可能已被调度器迁移到另一个节点（例如StatefulSet滚动更新场景）。原始代码在重试前没有重新检查Pod的节点信息，导致可能对已不在目标节点上的Pod进行不必要的驱逐尝试，甚至可能失败。新增的逻辑在429错误后重新获取Pod状态，如果Pod已不存在或节点已变更，则视为驱逐完成，从而避免不必要的操作和潜在错误。', 'evidence': '文件: staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/drain/drain.go
@@ -312,6 +312,28 @@ func (d *Helper) evictPods(pods []corev1.Pod, evictionGroupVersion schema.GroupV
 					//nolint:errcheck
 					fmt.Fprintf(d.ErrOut, "error when evicting pods/%q -n %q (will retry after %v): %v\n", activePod.Name, activePod.Namespace, d.EvictErrorRetryDelay, err)
 					time.Sleep(d.EvictErrorRetryDelay)
+					// Re-verify the pod state after 429 error to check if it has been migrated
+					freshPod, err := getPodFn(activePod.Namespace, activePod.Name)
+					if err != nil {
+						if apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
+							// Pod no longer exists on the source node; consider eviction complete
+							returnCh <- nil
+							return
+						}
+						// For other API errors, log and continue with the original pod
+						//nolint:errcheck
+						fmt.Fprintf(d.ErrOut, "warning: failed to re-verify pod %q -n %q after eviction error: %v\n", activePod.Name, activePod.Namespace, err)
+					} else if freshPod.Spec.NodeName != pod.Spec.NodeName {
+						// Pod has been migrated/rescheduled to a different node
+						// (common in StatefulSets); eviction is complete for the target node
+						//nolint:errcheck
+						fmt.Fprintf(d.Out, "pod %s/%s has been rescheduled to node %s, skipping eviction\n", freshPod.Namespace, freshPod.Name, freshPod.Spec.NodeName)
+						returnCh <- nil
+						return
+					} else {
+						// Pod is still on the target node, update and retry
+						activePod = *freshPod
+					}', 'advice': '逻辑正确，但建议将比较的基准节点从 `pod.Spec.NodeName` 改为 `activePod.Spec.NodeName`，因为 `activePod` 是循环中可能被更新的当前Pod对象，而 `pod` 是函数参数，在循环中未改变。虽然在这个上下文中它们初始值相同，但使用 `activePod` 更符合逻辑和代码意图。', 'confidence': 0.9, 'severity': 'Medium'}, {'type': 'Readability', 'conclusion': '新增了针对429错误和Pod迁移场景的单元测试。', 'description': '新增的测试函数 `TestEviction429WithPodMigration` 结构清晰，覆盖了Pod迁移到不同节点、Pod被删除以及Pod留在原节点三种主要场景，有助于验证代码变更的正确性。测试用例的描述（`description`）有助于理解测试目的。', 'evidence': '文件: staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/drain/drain_test.go
@@ -638,3 +638,139 @@ func TestEvictDuringNamespaceTerminating(t *testing.T) {
 	}
 }
 
+// TestEviction429WithPodMigration tests that kubectl drain handles 429 errors gracefully
+// and detects when pods have been migrated to different nodes.
+func TestEviction429WithPodMigration(t *testing.T) {
+	testCases := []struct {
+		name                    string
+		initialNodeName         string
+		migratedNodeName        string
+		podExistsAfterMigration bool
+		expectSuccess           bool
+		description             string
+	}{...}', 'advice': '测试代码良好，无需修改。', 'confidence': 1.0, 'severity': 'Low'}, {'type': 'Design', 'conclusion': '代码变更引入了对特定错误（429）后的Pod状态重新验证逻辑。', 'description': '为了处理Pod在收到429错误后可能迁移的情况，代码在429错误处理分支中嵌入了重新获取并验证Pod状态的逻辑。这改变了原有的错误处理流程，使得对于429错误，会立即尝试重新验证Pod状态，而对于其他错误，则是在后续的通用逻辑中刷新Pod状态。这种设计是合理的，因为它针对特定场景进行了优化。', 'evidence': '文件: staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/drain/drain.go
@@ -312,6 +312,28 @@ func (d *Helper) evictPods(pods []corev1.Pod, evictionGroupVersion schema.GroupV
 					//nolint:errcheck
 					fmt.Fprintf(d.ErrOut, "error when evicting pods/%q -n %q (will retry after %v): %v\n", activePod.Name, activePod.Namespace, d.EvictErrorRetryDelay, err)
 					time.Sleep(d.EvictErrorRetryDelay)
+					// Re-verify the pod state after 429 error to check if it has been migrated
+					freshPod, err := getPodFn(activePod.Namespace, activePod.Name)
+					if err != nil { ... } else if freshPod.Spec.NodeName != pod.Spec.NodeName { ... } else { ... }', 'advice': '设计符合场景需求。注意在比较节点名称时，建议使用 `activePod.Spec.NodeName` 而非 `pod.Spec.NodeName` 作为基准，以保持与循环内 `activePod` 变量更新的一致性。', 'confidence': 0.8, 'severity': 'Low'}]

commits:
[{'sha': 'c5715e7080d516ea8b67827d760f4fa85dfb03bc', 'message': 'Add Windows node-level e2e tests to a dedicated directory

Introduce test/e2e_node_windows/ as a self-contained Windows node e2e
test suite, fully isolated from the Linux-focused test/e2e_node/ package.
All new files are gated with //go:build windows.

Add //go:build linux tags to test/e2e_node/services/kubelet.go,
services/server.go, and builder/build.go to make their Linux/systemd
assumptions explicit and allow clean coexistence with the new Windows
counterparts.

Include the Windows test binary (e2e_node_windows.test) in the build
when KUBE_BUILD_PLATFORMS targets Windows in hack/lib/golang.sh.'}]

', '@zylxjtu At what point we say, windows kubelet is not a real kubelet? cc @kubernetes/sig-node-leads ', "> @zylxjtu At what point we say, windows kubelet is not a real kubelet? I don't really understand your comment. Are you saying that we should stop supporting windows (via sig-windows) in Kubenetes? I think this PR is mostly that e2e_node was geared around testing kubelet in linux. Even if this PR doesn't land I don't see that being a blocker for us supporting windows. ", '> > @zylxjtu At what point we say, windows kubelet is not a real kubelet? > > I don't really understand your comment. Are you saying that we should stop supporting windows (via sig-windows) in Kubenetes? > > I think this PR is mostly that e2e_node was geared around testing kubelet in linux. Even if this PR doesn't land I don't see that being a blocker for us supporting windows. I think the question (at least from my POV) is valid. Since Windows kubelet is lagging behind of more and more features, I really do not want to mix up the e2e_node infrastracture very tightly to whatever Windows behavior is. Thus my comments above on making it as detached as possible. I will be fine with the copy of e2e_node framework specifically for Windows (maintained completely separately). I am not ok to start accumulating debt of remembering that e2e_node framework code must run on Windows and all the limitations kubelet on windows imposes. Thus the question on whether windows kubelet is the same as non-windows one and whether it is even "conformant" with k8s. (btw, it may not be conformant by now as we promoting features like in-place resize and pod level resources and supporting them is a "conformance" requirement)', '[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *zylxjtu* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [thockin](https://github.com/thockin) for approval. For more information see [the Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=kubernetes%2Fkubernetes).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[hack/OWNERS](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/hack/OWNERS)** - **[test/OWNERS](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/test/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
', '@SergeyKanzhelev , regarding this "(btw, it may not be conformant by now as we promoting features like in-place resize and pod level resources and supporting them is a "conformance" requirement)", are there any link maintained by sig-node for a conformance feature list? Windows maybe behind as we are a relatively small community, but we are trying to catch as much as we can', '> @SergeyKanzhelev , regarding this > > "(btw, it may not be conformant by now as we promoting features like in-place resize and pod level resources and supporting them is a "conformance" requirement)", > > are there any link maintained by sig-node for a conformance feature list? Windows maybe behind as we are a relatively small community, but we are trying to catch as much as we can By no means it is a criticism of people working hard to support Windows. I understand the complexity of a task. That's said, there may be better way to handle Windows. When I said conformance - I mean real conformance tests defined in `test/conformance/testdata/conformance.yaml`. My understanding is that the attempt to run conformance was closed before entering alpha: https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/issues/2578. And I am pointing out the fact that windows kubelet is moving further away from being conformant with each feature.', '@zylxjtu: The following tests **failed**, say `/retest` to rerun all failed tests or `/retest-required` to rerun all mandatory failed tests: Test name | Commit | Details | Required | Rerun command --- | --- | --- | --- | --- pull-kubernetes-typecheck | c5715e7080d516ea8b67827d760f4fa85dfb03bc | [link](https://prow.k8s.io/view/gs/kubernetes-ci-logs/pr-logs/pull/138235/pull-kubernetes-typecheck/2041265741684019200) | true | `/test pull-kubernetes-typecheck` pull-kubernetes-linter-hints | c5715e7080d516ea8b67827d760f4fa85dfb03bc | [link](https://prow.k8s.io/view/gs/kubernetes-ci-logs/pr-logs/pull/138235/pull-kubernetes-linter-hints/2041265743487569920) | false | `/test pull-kubernetes-linter-hints` pull-kubernetes-verify | c5715e7080d516ea8b67827d760f4fa85dfb03bc | [link](https://prow.k8s.io/view/gs/kubernetes-ci-logs/pr-logs/pull/138235/pull-kubernetes-verify/2041265742636126208) | true | `/test pull-kubernetes-verify` pull-kubernetes-node-kubelet-serial-containerd | c5715e7080d516ea8b67827d760f4fa85dfb03bc | [link](https://prow.k8s.io/view/gs/kubernetes-ci-logs/pr-logs/pull/138235/pull-kubernetes-node-kubelet-serial-containerd/2041265740610277376) | false | `/test pull-kubernetes-node-kubelet-serial-containerd` [Full PR test history](https://prow.k8s.io/pr-history?org=kubernetes&repo=kubernetes&pr=138235). review: [{'type': 'Bug', 'conclusion': '为字符串字段添加正则表达式模式验证功能', 'description': '测试用例中期望的错误信息与实际生成的错误信息不匹配。测试用例期望的错误信息使用了空字符串作为错误值，但实际函数会返回原始值。这会导致测试失败。', 'evidence': '在 pattern_test.go 中，多个测试用例的 `wantErrs` 使用了 `field.Invalid(field.NewPath("fldPath"), "", "")`，但 `Pattern` 函数会返回 `field.Invalid(fldPath, *value, "value does not match regex pattern")`，其中 `*value` 是实际值（如 "abc123"），而不是空字符串。', 'advice': '修改测试用例中的期望错误信息，使其与实际生成的错误信息匹配。例如，将 `field.Invalid(field.NewPath("fldPath"), "", "")` 改为 `field.Invalid(field.NewPath("fldPath"), "abc123", "value does not match regex pattern")`。', 'confidence': 0.9, 'severity': 'High'}, {'type': 'Readability', 'conclusion': '为字符串字段添加正则表达式模式验证功能', 'description': '`Pattern` 函数的参数命名不够清晰，特别是 `value` 和 `_`（旧值）参数。使用 `_` 作为参数名会降低代码可读性，因为读者需要查看函数签名才能理解其含义。', 'evidence': '`func Pattern[T ~string](_ context.Context, _ operation.Operation, fldPath *field.Path, value, _ *T, pattern *regexp.Regexp) field.ErrorList`', 'advice': '建议为旧值参数赋予一个有意义的名称，例如 `oldValue`，即使它可能不被使用，这有助于提高代码的可读性和一致性。', 'confidence': 0.7, 'severity': 'Low'}, {'type': 'Design', 'conclusion': '为字符串字段添加正则表达式模式验证功能', 'description': '`Pattern` 函数对 `nil` 的 `pattern` 参数没有进行防御性检查。如果传入的 `pattern` 为 `nil`，调用 `pattern.MatchString` 会导致运行时 panic。', 'evidence': '`if !pattern.MatchString(string(*value)) {` 这一行直接使用了 `pattern` 指针，没有检查其是否为 `nil`。', 'advice': '应在函数开始处检查 `pattern` 是否为 `nil`。如果是 `nil`，可以返回一个错误（例如 `field.InternalError`），或者 panic（如果这是编程错误）。考虑到这是内部生成的代码，且模式在编译时验证，panic 可能是合理的选择，但显式处理会使函数更健壮。', 'confidence': 0.8, 'severity': 'Medium'}]
commits:
[{'sha': '980f94599ac7de79e49727fc016d3d98a0850b25', 'message': "validation-gen: add +k8s:pattern declarative validation tag

Add a new +k8s:pattern=<regex> declarative validation tag that validates
a string field matches the specified regular expression pattern. This
corresponds to the OpenAPI/JSON Schema pattern keyword.

Changes:
- Add Pattern[T ~string] runtime validation function to
  k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validate with 'pattern' origin.
  Accepts a pre-compiled *regexp.Regexp for performance.
- Add TestPattern to pattern_test.go covering nil, matching,
  non-matching, empty string, and multiple pattern cases.
- Add patternTagValidator to validation-gen validators/pattern.go:
  - Restricted to string types only (consistent with +k8s:format,
    +k8s:maxLength)
  - Validates regex at code generation time (lint error on bad pattern)
  - Compiles regexp once as a package-level variable via VariableGen
    (no per-call compilation)
  - Uses Literal() to safely pass regex strings containing '$' to the
    SnippetWriter template engine
  - Valid scopes: ScopeType, ScopeField, ScopeListVal, ScopeMapKey,
    ScopeMapVal
  - Stability level: Alpha
  - Documents RE2 engine constraints (no lookahead/backreferences)
- Add output_tests/tags/pattern with doc.go, doc_test.go, and
  zz_generated.validations.go covering field-attached, type-attached,
  pointer, unvalidated typedef, validated typedef, and ratcheting cases.
- Add CHANGELOG entry.

Fixes: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/138116
Signed-off-by: sAchin-680 <mrmister680@gmail.com>"}]

commits:
[{'sha': '7fdccaf166241226858c2fb0ab9ae960c9db276e', 'message': "Recreate deployment: resync when only terminal pods remain

Previously, the Recreate deployment strategy only resynced in deletePod
when there were zero pods across all ReplicaSets. This caused the
rollout to stall when terminated pods (Succeeded/Failed) still existed
but hadn't been fully cleaned up yet.

Change deletePod to skip terminal pods when deciding whether to enqueue
a resync, and extract isPodTerminalForRecreate as a shared helper used
by both deletePod and oldPodsRunning.

Ref: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/106410"}]
review_comments:
['Can also consider: - [IsPodTerminal](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/6c1cd99aef09161ddb07b8ade6c9564e9b9bfe27/pkg/api/v1/pod/util.go#L302) If it is safe to remove the `PodUnknown` check', "@raghavan-arvind: The label(s) `area/deployment` cannot be applied, because the repository doesn't have them.
In response to [this](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/138231): >## Summary > >- Fix Recreate deployment strategy to resync in `deletePod` when only terminal pods (Succeeded/Failed) remain, instead of waiting for zero pods total >- Extract `isPodTerminalForRecreate` helper to share pod phase logic between `deletePod` and `oldPodsRunning` >- Add unit test covering the terminal-pods-only scenario > >Fixes https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/106410 > >## Details > >Previously, the `deletePod` handler for Recreate deployments counted all pods and only enqueued a resync when `numPods == 0`. This meant that if old pods were in a terminal phase (Succeeded/Failed) but hadn't been garbage collected yet, the rollout would stall waiting for them to disappear. > >Now `deletePod` uses the same terminal-pod logic as `oldPodsRunning` in `recreate.go`, skipping pods in Failed/Succeeded phase. Pods in PodUnknown are conservatively treated as active since we can't confirm they've stopped. > >## Test plan > >- [x] Existing unit tests pass (`go test ./pkg/controller/deployment/`) >- [x] New test `TestPodDeletionEnqueuesRecreateDeploymentWithTerminalPods` verifies resync is triggered when only terminal pods remain > >/sig apps >/kind bug >/area deployment Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).
